Experiential design
at a luxury standard.

Every experience documented here was conceived, designed, and produced without an outside creative director or production company. The creative vision, the design direction, and every production decision and execution were mine alone.

Event 01

The Iberville Ballroom Reveal

Hotel Monteleone | New Orleans, Louisiana | November 8, 2022

Hotel Monteleone had just completed a full remodel of the Iberville Ballroom. The goal was not an announcement. It was a proof of concept, delivered live to the people whose bookings would define the ballroom's future.

I conceived and produced a luxury murder mystery dinner experience for the city's top wedding planners, designing every element in-house from concept to execution. At the end of the evening, every planner in the room wanted to know which production company had done it. The answer was: no one. This was built from a blank page with internal resources.

Every top wedding planner in New Orleans left that night having experienced the ballroom at its best, and knowing exactly what it could do for their clients. This was not part of my job description. I conceived it, pitched it, and executed it as a self-initiated project to test what was possible with internal resources alone.

View Mood Board
Event 02

Feast of the Senses

Hotel Monteleone x Madame Aucoin Perfume x Criollo | New Orleans, LouisianaSeptember 17, 2025

Five courses. Five scents. One question: what does perfume taste like?

The culinary concept and fragrance pairings had already been built by the chef and Madame Aucoin. What did not exist was the experiential world around them. I was brought in to solve that problem.

I assessed their food and beverage presentation alongside the fragrance collection and conceived an apothecary-inspired, botanical aesthetic that unified every element of the evening into a single sensory language. I then produced that vision from the ground up: the florals, the place cards, the menus, the table decor, the event signage, and the full atmospheric design. I translated their culinary and olfactory vision into an immersive experience guests could see, smell, taste, and feel simultaneously.

The event was ticketed and sold out. The social coverage was organic. The partnership positioned the hotel as a creative force in the New Orleans luxury market, not just a venue for other people's events.
